meta.description: A typed, multi-runtime function definition. The contract (name, return type, runtime) lives in the item; the implementation lives in a deployment bundle on disk and in cloud storage. Per the flat one-level rule the function's parameters are `parameter` children, its generic type parameters are `type-parameter` children, and its declared exceptions are `function-throw` children (all ordered by item.sortOrder); bundleHash is stored as `property` children (item.value = runtime name, payload.value = hash) — never inline arrays/objects on this payload.

runtime.description: Execution runtime for this function. Open string — new runtimes can be added without a spec change. The runtime name is also the subdirectory name inside the .function/ bundle directory. Defaults to 'typescript' if absent. The runtime name must be a valid directory name (lowercase letters, hyphens, digits).

ai.description: Whether the function body was AI-generated.

skillId.description: UUID of the skill item this function belongs to, if any. Exposed on the read/write payload as `skill`.

returnType.description: Primitive return type string in the target runtime's type system (e.g. 'string', 'number' for TypeScript). Exactly one of returnType or returnTypeId must be present.

returnTypeId.description: UUID of a Kanecta type item for object-typed return values. Runtime-neutral — the adapter maps the type's jsonSchema to the appropriate declaration. Exactly one of returnType or returnTypeId must be present.

deprecated.description: If set, marks the function as deprecated. Value is the deprecation message.

body.description: Inline function body source. Used by runtimes/adapters that store the implementation on the item rather than in an on-disk bundle (e.g. Postgres, where meta.files is not available).

includeKanectaSdk.description: Whether the generated scaffold imports the Kanecta SDK. Defaults to true when absent.

dependencies.description: Package dependencies declared for this function (e.g. ["axios@^1.0.0"] for TypeScript/npm). These are merged into the generated package.json by the adapter. The package.json in the bundle is the authoritative source for installed dependencies; this field is the spec-level declaration used when scaffolding.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
